title: Telangana Chicken Curry

subs.name: 750 g mutton; increase simmer time to 35 minutes
0.5 tsp extra red chilli powder; hotter but the peppery aroma is lost

steps.text: Mix the chicken with turmeric, half the chilli powder and a pinch of salt. Set aside while preparing the base masala.

Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot or kadai over medium-high flame. Add dried red chillies and curry leaves; sizzle 20 seconds. Add the onion and fry, stirring every 2 minutes, until it turns a rich dark brown, about 15 minutes. This long caramelisation is the backbone of Telangana cooking.
Add ginger-garlic paste and cook until the raw smell leaves, about 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and cook until they completely disintegrate and the oil separates from the masala, about 6 minutes.
Lower the flame, add the remaining chilli powder, coriander powder and black pepper; stir for 30 seconds. The black pepper is a key Telangana flavour note — do not reduce it.
Add the marinated chicken and stir to coat in the masala. Increase to high heat and sear, turning, until the chicken is no longer pink on the outside, about 5 minutes.
Add 250 ml hot water and salt. Cover and cook on medium heat for 20 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked and the gravy has thickened.
Uncover, add garam masala and toss on high heat for 3–4 minutes to reduce the curry to a semi-dry, thick-coated consistency. Finish with coriander leaves and rest 5 minutes before serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → 750 g mutton; increase simmer time to 35 minutes; black-pepper → 0.5 tsp extra red chilli powder; hotter but the peppery aroma is lost
